events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The iconic Rolling Stones frontman, Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent, England.

angles.name: Mick's Enduring Energy
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still commanding stages worldwide! Today we celebrate the legendary Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Rolling Stones track to get you moving?

events.title: MTV launched, playing 'Video Killed The Radio Star'
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first music video.

angles.name: The Day The Video Star Was Born
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV changed music forever, kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony isn't lost on us. What was the first music video that blew your mind?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe'
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became the fastest-selling debut album in British history at the time.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: It's been 32 years since Oasis dropped their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe'. An instant classic that defined a generation. What's your go-to track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music and Art Fair took place from August 15 to 18, 1969, on a dairy farm in Bethel, New York, attracting over 400,000 people for '3 Days of Peace & Music'.

angles.name: Peace, Love, and Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for Woodstock, a defining moment of peace, love, and music. Which iconic performance from the festival do you wish you'd seen live?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977. He would have been 91 in 2026, having been born in 1935.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember Elvis Presley, who left us 49 years ago. His music and legacy continue to inspire generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur was tragically shot dead in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996. He would have been 55 in 2026, having been born in 1971.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Thirty years ago, the music world lost a profound voice. Today, we remember Tupac Shakur and his enduring impact on hip-hop and culture. What's your favourite Tupac track?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time, died on September 18, 1970. He would have been 84 in 2026, having been born in 1942.

angles.name: Jimi Hendrix Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix, who left us too soon 56 years ago today. His guitar mastery continues to inspire. What's your favourite Hendrix riff?

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road'
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, and in the US on October 1, 1969.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That iconic album cover and timeless tracks. What's your favourite from this legendary record?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' lead singer, Chrissie Hynde, was born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio, USA.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the rock icon Chrissie Hynde! The voice and force behind The Pretenders. What's your favourite track from her incredible career?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester, England.

angles.name: Mad For It Birthday
angles.copy: Our kid turns 54 today! Happy Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher. What's your favourite Liam moment or lyric?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released as a single
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic song 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, and the album was released in the UK on October 8, 1971.

angles.name: Imagine's Message
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon gave the world 'Imagine', a timeless anthem for peace and unity. A message that still resonates today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music.

angles.name: The Click Wheel Revolution
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, Apple dropped the first iPod, changing how we listen to music forever. Who remembers the pure joy of the click wheel?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first James Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From the gadgets to the theme tunes, 007 is pure cinematic gold. Who's your favourite Bond and why?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Lennon, the legendary co-founder of The Beatles, was born on October 9, 1940, and would have been 86 today.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message continue to inspire peace and love globally. Which Lennon song resonates most with you?

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese was born on October 27, 1939, in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, England.

angles.name: Comedy Legend's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 87th Birthday to the one and only John Cleese! A true titan of British comedy, from Monty Python to Fawlty Towers. What's your favourite John Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album'
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-eight years ago, The Beatles unleashed 'The White Album' – a sprawling, diverse masterpiece. Which track from this double LP stands out for you?
